revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:岩戸山古墳]][[zh:岩戶山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Iwatoyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|qid=Q64615207|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Yame|qid=Q850492|1=Yame}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.229858|N|130.552714|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-40 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | built = Early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} | burial = Unknown | map_size = Mound length: 135m | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Iwatoyama Kofun" (Included in the "Yame Kofun Group") | burial goods = Stone burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, etc. | Interred_individual = (Presumed) {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}} | image = File:Iwatoyama-kofun enkei.JPG | notes = Largest in northern {{ill|Kyushu region|ja|九州地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州|1=Kyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13987}}
Mentioned in an excerpt from the {{ill|Chikugo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|筑後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikugo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505754|1=Chikugo-no-Kuni Fudoki}}
A tumulus with burial candidate and construction period estimations based on literature | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|coordinates=33|coordinates=13|coordinates=47.49|lon_deg=130|lon_min=33|lon_sec=9.77|map_dot_label= Iwatoyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Fukuoka Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|13|47.49|N|130|33|9.77|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Tsukushi no Iwai|qid=Q11602917|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}} }} {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|qid=Q850492|lt=Yame, Fukuoka|1=Yame}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|qid=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic site as a National Historic site (included within the "Yame Kofun Group" Historic site). It is the largest kofun in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州}}The largest kofun in Kyushu is {{ill|Nyōsazuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyōsazuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11446520}} in Miyazaki Prefecture, with a total length of 176m. and is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. == Overview == {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}} is situated in the northern area of Yame City on the Yame Hills. These hills span approximately 10 kilometers east to west and are home to numerous kofun dating from the 5th to the 6th centuries.While most kofun here date from the 5th to 6th centuries, the range includes square moated tombs from the early 4th century to circular kofun from the early 7th century ("Iwatoyama Historical Museum Exhibit Catalog," p. 9). Among the approximately 300 Kofuns, including 12 keyhole-shaped kofun and three decorated kofun, Iwatoyama Kofun is one of the most prominent.“Iwatoyama Historical Museum Exhibit Catalog,” p. 9. The kofun is oriented along an east-west axis, with its circular rear mound facing east. It features a two-tiered construction and includes a unique square section known as "Bekkū" (別区), measuring 43 meters on each side."Iwatoyama Historical Museum Exhibit Catalog," p. 16. Based on the dating of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, it is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century, aligning with historical records of {{ill|Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. Although the internal structure is unknown, geophysical surveys suggest the presence of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. A shrine (Daijingū) stands near the tumulus, which originally occupied the top of the circular mound. The tumulus, surrounding moat, and Bekkū have yielded over 100 stone burial goods, collectively referred to as "stone humans and horses" ({{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|qid=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}}). These burial goods, which are unique to the area, fall into three categories: human figures, animals, and utensils. Most are life-sized. Replicas of these stone burial goods, map_sized down, are now displayed in Bekkū. Iwatoyama Kofun was Historic site as a National Historic site on December 23, 1955. The Historic site was expanded and unified into the "Yame Kofun Group" on March 24, 1978.“Illustrated Guide to Japan’s Historic sites, Volume 3” (Dōhōsha Publishing, 1991), p. 206.The historic sites “Iwatoyama Kofun,” “{{ill|Noriba Kofun|lt=Noriba Kofun|lt=Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobaba Kofun|qq=}},” “{{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}},” “{{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}},” and “{{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}” were unified, with three additional kofun ({{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17503167}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Yame)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}) added. == Kofun area == * Total length: Over 170 meters * Mound length: Addroximately 135 meters * Circular rear mound: ''' Diameter: Addroximately 60 meters ''' Height: Addroximately 18 meters * Rectangular front mound: ''' Width: Addroximately 92 meters ''' Height: Addroximately 17 meters * Bekkū: ''' Square area with 43-meter sides A moat and embankment 20 meters wide surround the tumulus. == burial == The interred is believed to be {{ill|Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, a regional ruler of northern Kyushu in the early 6th century. This kofun is one of the few in Japan where burial and construction dates can be estimated based on historical documents. {{ill|Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}} is noted in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} for leading a rebellion (see {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|qid=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}}). The {{ill|Chikugo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|筑後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikugo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505754|1=Chikugo-no-Kuni Fudoki}} fragment describes the kofun and its location, including references to judicial-like functions in Bekkū. According to this account, Iwai began constructing his tomb during his lifetime but abandoned it after losing a battle. Previously, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}} was thought to be Iwai's tomb. This was revised by scholar Sadashirō Mori in 1956, and Iwatoyama Kofun is now widely accepted as his final resting place. == Related Cultural Assets == * Stone human figure (Tokyo National Museum, Important designation1)Ministry of Education, Science, and Culture Notification No. 106, June 5, 1976. * Two stone human figures, one stone quiver, and two stone shields with attached fragments (Yame City, stored at Iwatoyama Historical Cultural Exchange Hall, Important designation1)Ministry of Education, Science, and Culture Notification No. 108, June 29, 2010. * Stone horse, armored human head, and two stone human fragments (traditionally from Iwatoyama Kofun, owned by Shōfuku-ji, Yame City, and Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1)[http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/1496969698028.html "Cultural Assets of Yame City"] (Yame City website, accessed December 17, 2020). == Gallery == File:Iwatoyama-kofun Daijinguu.JPG|Shrine near the tumulus File:Iwatoyama-kofun kouenbu.JPG|Former site of the shrine at the rear mound File:Iwatoyama-kofun bekku.JPG|Bekkū with stone replicas File:Iwatoyama-kofun ishiseihin.JPG|Replica of stone burial goods in Bekkū File:岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|Stone Shield (Owned by Yame City, preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center, Important designation1) File:岩戸山古墳 石盾 2.jpg|Stone Shield (Owned by Yame City, preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center, Important designation1) File:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 2.jpg|Stone Figure (Owned by Yame City, preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center, Important designation1) == Related Facilities == * Iwatoyama Historical Cultural Exchange Hall (Iwai no Sato)[http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/iwatoyama/ Official website] ''' location: 1562-1 Yoshida, Yame City ''' Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:15 PM ''' Closed: Mondays == Transportation == * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|qid=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station|1=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629843}} bound for Yame Office and alight at "Fukushima High School," then walk 4 minutes (350 meters). * By car: 6 kilometers from {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1654393}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|qid=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}. == notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|qid=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Iwatoyama-kofun}} * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/iwatoyama/index.html Iwatoyama Historical Cultural Exchange Hall Official Website] * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2678|Yame Kofun Group}} 岩戸山古墳]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|岩戸山古墳|version=103640966|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:岩戶山古墳]] [[ja:岩戸山古墳]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== '''Iwatoyama Kofun''' (岩戸山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|qid=Q850492|lt=Yame, Fukuoka|1=Yame}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|qid=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}} and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} (under the collective designation "Yame Kofun Cluster"). Iwatoyama Kofun is the largest tumulus in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州}}, estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} during the {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q459939}}.The largest tumulus in Kyushu is the {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofun|lt=Onasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳}} in {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, with a mound length of 176 meters. == Overview == [[File:Iwatoyama-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of Iwatoyama Kofun}}{{small|Foreground: keyhole front section, Background: round rear section.}}]] [[File:Iwatoyama-kofun Daijinguu.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Daijinguu shrine adjacent to the tumulus.}}]] Iwatoyama Kofun is situated in the northern part of Yame City on the Yame Hills, which extend for over 10 kilometers. These hills are home to numerous tumuli constructed between the {{ill|5th and 6th centuries|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th and 6th centuries|qq=}}.While the majority of tumuli date from the 5th to 6th centuries, earlier structures like square moated graves from the early 4th century and circular tumuli from the early 7th century are also present (per the *Iwatoyama Historical Museum Exhibition Catalog*, p. 9). The cluster includes approximately 300 tumuli, including 12 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 3 decorated tumuli, with Iwatoyama Kofun being one of the most prominent.*Iwatoyama Historical Museum Exhibition Catalog*, p. 9. The tumulus, oriented along an east-west axis, features a two-tier construction with a unique square annex area, known as the "Bekku" (別区), measuring 43 meters on each side, located at the northeast corner.*Iwatoyama Historical Museum Exhibition Catalog*, p. 16. Based on the dating of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, it is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century, aligning with historical records regarding the presumed interred individual, {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. Subsurface exploration has suggested the presence of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, although the burial facilities have not been fully excavated. A shrine, Daijinguu, is situated beside the tumulus, and it was originally located at the summit of the round section of the mound. More than 100 stone artifacts collectively referred to as {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|qid=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} have been unearthed from the tumulus, surrounding embankments, and the annex area. These artifacts, unique to the region, depict human figures, animals, and tools, and are notable for their life-size proportions. Today, replicas of these stone artifacts, scaled down in size, are displayed in the annex area. Iwatoyama Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site on December 23, 1955. In 1978, additional tumuli were included under a collective designation as the "Yame Kofun Cluster."*Illustrated Guide to Japanese Historic Sites, Vol. 3* (Dōbōsha Shu11an, 1991), p. 206.The redesignation combined five previously designated historic sites (Iwatoyama Kofun, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}) with three additional tumuli ({{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17503167}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Yame)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Mound Details == * Total length: over 170 meters * Mound length: approximately 135 meters * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: approximately 60 meters ''' Height: approximately 18 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: approximately 92 meters ''' Height: approximately 17 meters * Annex area: ''' Square shape with sides measuring 43 meters The tumulus is surrounded by a 20-meter-wide moat and embankment. == Interred_individual == The buried individual is believed to be '''{{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}''', a prominent ruler of northern Kyushu in the early 6th century. Iwatoyama Kofun is one of the few tumuli in Japan where historical records corroborate the identity of the buried individual and the construction period. Chikushi Kimi Iwai is known for the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|qid=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}}, described in the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}* and *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}*. Additionally, fragments from the *{{ill|Chikugo Fudoki|ja|筑後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikugo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505754|1=Chikugo-no-Kuni Fudoki}}* provide descriptions of the tumulus and its annex, suggesting the site may have been used for judicial purposes. According to these texts, Iwai began constructing the tomb during his lifetime but abandoned it after losing a battle. Historically, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}} was considered Iwai's tomb. However, in 1956, scholar Sadashiro Mori identified Iwatoyama Kofun as the burial site, a view that has since become the consensus. == Related Cultural Properties == * Stone human figure ({{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} collection, {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}})Official Gazette, Ministry of Education Notification No. 106, June 5, 1976 * Stone artifacts including two human figures, one quiver, two shields, and 57 accompanying fragments (owned by Yame City, housed at Iwatoyama History and Culture Exchange Center, Important Cultural Property)Official Gazette, Ministry of Education Notification No. 108, June 29, 2010 * Stone horse, armored stone human head, and two human figure fragments (owned by Shōfukuji Temple, housed at Iwatoyama History and Culture Exchange Center, Fukuoka Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property)[http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/1496969698028.html Yame City Cultural Properties] (Accessed December 17, 2020) == Gallery == File:Iwatoyama-kofun ishiseihin.JPG|Stone artifacts in the annex area (replicas) File:Iwatoyama-kofun kouenbu.JPG|Rear circular section of the mound File:Iwatoyama-kofun bekku.JPG|Annex area (Bekku) with stone artifacts File:Iwatoyama-kofun Daijinguu.JPG|Daijinguu shrine next to the tumulus File:岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|Stone shield (Important Cultural Property) File:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 2.jpg|Stone human figure (Important Cultural Property) == Related Facilities == * '''Iwatoyama History and Culture Exchange Center "Iwaino Sato"'''[http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/iwatoyama/index.html Official Site] ** Address: Yame City, Yoshida 1562-1 ** Opening hours: 9:00 AM - 5:15 PM ** Closed: Mondays == Access == * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|qid=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station|1=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629843}} bound for Yame Depot and alight at "Fukushima High School," then walk 4 minutes (350 meters). * By car: 6 km from the {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1654393}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|qid=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|qid=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Iwatoyama-kofun}} * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/iwatoyama/index.html Official Site of Iwatoyama History and Culture Exchange Center] * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2678|Yame Kofun Cluster}} {{translated page|ja|岩戸山古墳|version=103640966|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:岩戸山古墳]] {{Commons category|Iwatoyama Kofun}} {{Commons category|Iwatoyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11474582}} [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
